# Claim 3: CIFAR-10 Accuracy


---
<!-- trackio-cell
{"type": "markdown", "id": "cell_f2b3f82f9f9e", "created_at": "2026-07-17T19:38:08+00:00", "title": "Claim and current verdict"}
-->
Exact claim: On the held-in CIFAR-10 task, ML-Agent achieves an average accuracy of 68.88% with a best run of 81.45% (Table 1).

Verdict: INCONCLUSIVE/BLOCKED pending a GPU run (score 0/2). The values 68.88% and 81.45% are paper-derived and are not plotted or relabeled as measured results. Exact rerun is blocked by the absent ML-Agent checkpoint and evaluator. A scaled CIFAR-10 run will be reported separately and cannot validate these exact ML-Agent statistics.


---
<!-- trackio-cell
{"type": "markdown", "id": "cell_69fb291d6de4", "created_at": "2026-07-17T19:47:06+00:00", "title": "Hugging Face A10G five-seed run submitted"}
-->
Substantive CIFAR-10 rerun: https://huggingface.co/jobs/binzhango/6a5a8688d216bd6f3a1fc070. Hardware: one Nvidia A10G (24 GB), a10g-small. Protocol: five unique seeds, 12 epochs each, all 50,000 training images and all 10,000 test images, fresh CifarConvNet weights. Timeout: three hours; maximum hardware charge 3.00 USD at the current 1.00 USD/hour rate. Persistent output mount: https://huggingface.co/buckets/binzhango/ml-agent-repro-v2-jobs#cifar-gpu. Initial status after submission: RUNNING. This remains a task/model proxy and is not the unreleased ML-Agent checkpoint.


---
<!-- trackio-cell
{"type": "markdown", "id": "cell_62c683ee1263", "created_at": "2026-07-17T20:00:34+00:00", "title": "Instrumented A10G retry with Bucket-staged CIFAR-10"}
-->
The first A10G Job reached CUDA initialization but emitted no dataset or epoch event for approximately 11 minutes and was canceled to stop unproductive charges: https://huggingface.co/jobs/binzhango/6a5a8688d216bd6f3a1fc070. CIFAR-10 parquet was staged at https://huggingface.co/buckets/binzhango/ml-agent-repro-v2-jobs#datasets/cifar10. Retry https://huggingface.co/jobs/binzhango/6a5a89d5bee6ee1cf4ecddc7 reads the staged parquet, uses zero DataLoader workers, and prints dataset, batch, epoch, and seed markers. Outputs persist in the automatic jobs-artifacts Bucket.


---
<!-- trackio-cell
{"type": "code", "id": "cell_c5a9749c85e4", "created_at": "2026-07-18T01:22:24+00:00", "title": "Fail-closed verification of downloaded A10G five-seed results", "command": ["rtk", "env", "UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache", "uv", "run", "scripts/verify_cifar_repeats.py", "--results", "outputs/hf_jobs/cifar-gpu/results.json"], "exit_code": 0, "duration_s": 0.087}
-->
````bash
$ rtk env UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run scripts/verify_cifar_repeats.py --results outputs/hf_jobs/cifar-gpu/results.json
````

exit 0 · 0.1s


````python title=verify_cifar_repeats.py
# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.11"
# ///
"""Fail-closed checks for substantive CIFAR-10 evidence."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import math
from pathlib import Path


def require(condition: bool, message: str) -> None:
    if not condition:
        raise AssertionError(message)
    print(f"PASS: {message}")


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    parser.add_argument("--results", type=Path, required=True)
    args = parser.parse_args()
    data = json.loads(args.results.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    protocol = data["protocol"]
    measurement = data["measurement"]
    runs = measurement["runs"]
    require(protocol["dataset"] == "CIFAR-10", "dataset is real CIFAR-10")
    require(not protocol["synthetic"], "synthetic smoke data are rejected")
    require(protocol["device"] == "cuda" and protocol["gpu_name"], "substantive run used a named GPU")
    require(protocol["train_limit"] == 50000, "all 50,000 CIFAR-10 training examples were used")
    require(len(runs) >= 5 and len(set(protocol["seeds"])) == len(runs), "at least five unique-seed runs")
    require(all(run["test_samples"] == 10000 for run in runs), "every run evaluated all 10,000 test examples")
    require(all(math.isfinite(run["final_accuracy"]) and 0 <= run["final_accuracy"] <= 1 for run in runs), "all accuracies are finite and bounded")
    require(math.isfinite(measurement["mean_accuracy"]), "mean accuracy is newly measured")
    require(math.isfinite(measurement["sample_sd"]), "run-to-run sample standard deviation is reported")
    print("ALL_CIFAR_VERIFICATIONS_PASS")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

````


````json title=results.json
{
  "protocol": {
    "label": "scaled_cifar10_proxy_not_ml_agent",
    "dataset": "CIFAR-10",
    "synthetic": false,
    "model": "CifarConvNet (fresh training; not ML-Agent/Qwen2.5-7B)",
    "epochs": 12,
    "train_limit": 50000,
    "dataset_source_dir": "/inputs/datasets/cifar10",
    "seeds": [
      17897,
      17898,
      17899,
      17900,
      17901
    ],
    "device": "cuda",
    "gpu_name": "NVIDIA A10G"
  },
  "measurement": {
    "mean_accuracy": 0.8669,
    "sample_sd": 0.0012529964086141839,
    "best_run_accuracy": 0.868,
    "worst_run_accuracy": 0.8653,
    "ci95_half_width": 0.0010982997769279719,
    "runs": [
      {
        "seed": 17897,
        "final_accuracy": 0.868,
        "best_epoch_accuracy": 0.868,
        "test_samples": 10000,
        "wall_seconds": 770.2633622650001
      },
      {
        "seed": 17898,
        "final_accuracy": 0.8658,
        "best_epoch_accuracy": 0.8658,
        "test_samples": 10000,
        "wall_seconds": 765.703955893
      },
      {
        "seed": 17899,
        "final_accuracy": 0.8678,
        "best_epoch_accuracy": 0.8678,
        "test_samples": 10000,
        "wall_seconds": 768.6867657140001
      },
      {
        "seed": 17900,
        "final_accuracy": 0.8653,
        "best_epoch_accuracy": 0.8653,
        "test_samples": 10000,
        "wall_seconds": 766.4373543229995
      },
      {
        "seed": 17901,
        "final_accuracy": 0.8676,
        "best_epoch_accuracy": 0.8676,
        "test_samples": 10000,
        "wall_seconds": 767.2020282789999
      }
    ]
  },
  "environment": {
    "python": "3.12.12",
    "torch": "2.13.0+cu130",
    "cuda": "13.0"
  }
}

````


````output
PASS: dataset is real CIFAR-10
PASS: synthetic smoke data are rejected
PASS: substantive run used a named GPU
PASS: all 50,000 CIFAR-10 training examples were used
PASS: at least five unique-seed runs
PASS: every run evaluated all 10,000 test examples
PASS: all accuracies are finite and bounded
PASS: mean accuracy is newly measured
PASS: run-to-run sample standard deviation is reported
ALL_CIFAR_VERIFICATIONS_PASS

````


---
<!-- trackio-cell
{"type": "markdown", "id": "cell_fa964cea6128", "created_at": "2026-07-18T01:22:55+00:00", "title": "Completed A10G result: 86.69% mean over five seeds"}
-->
Verdict update: PARTIAL/PROXY. The exact claim is: On the held-in CIFAR-10 task, ML-Agent achieves an average accuracy of 68.88% with a best run of 81.45% (Table 1). A fresh A10G Job trained five independent CifarConvNet runs on all 50,000 CIFAR-10 training examples and evaluated every run on all 10,000 test examples. Final accuracies were tightly concentrated: mean 86.690%, sample SD 0.125 percentage points, 95% CI half-width 0.110 points, best 86.800%, and worst 86.530%. Job: https://huggingface.co/jobs/binzhango/6a5a8a51bee6ee1cf4ecddcb. Persistent artifacts: https://huggingface.co/buckets/binzhango/jobs-artifacts#20260717T200224-7699dd/cifar-gpu-localcopy. Runtime was 3,881 seconds on one A10G, approximately 1.08 USD at the listed 1.00 USD/hour rate. This directly reproduces repeated full-data CIFAR-10 measurement and exceeds the reported numbers, but it does not reproduce ML-Agent's agent-selected code or unreleased Qwen2.5-7B checkpoint; therefore numerical superiority must not be interpreted as confirming Claim 3 exactly.
